one_line_thesis: Assurant is a partner-distributed specialty insurer with leading positions in mobile device protection and lender-placed homeowners insurance.

deep_dive.executive_summary: Assurant is a partner-distributed specialty insurer with leading positions in mobile device protection and lender-placed homeowners insurance. FY2025 net income per share is about $17.09; at ~$275 (July 2026), total synthesis implies 8.07% per year over 7 years on filing-based owner cash. Moat is stable (embedded distribution, switching costs) but not wide; partial dhando because housing catastrophes and mobile partner concentration cap the bear case. Proof-first component schedule sums to ~$255 per share versus ~$275 price. Marvin proposes watch on math (below ~15% accumulate hurdle); [HUM...

pricing_analysis.conclusion: Hurdle prices are derived from the decision-grade universal contract; they do not constitute a capital decision.

decision.next_action: Freeze the evidence packet and select three method-independent raters.

low.rationale: Lifestyle ~48% of segment EBITDA; Connected Living mobile and financial services drive growth.

low.rationale: Fade as mobile attach rates mature and housing policies normalize.

low.rationale: Specialty insurance, catastrophe, and partner concentration risk premium.

low.rationale: Specialty insurer / warranty administrator peer multiples on normalized owner cash.

change_control.rule: A source fact is locked. Every assumption change requires a reason, author, timestamp, and before/after value.

optionality.rule: Every option requires a beneficiary, probability, timing, remaining capital, failure case, and non-overlap control; absence of option value must be explicit rather than unvalued.

committee.next_action: Freeze the evidence packet and select three method-independent raters.

method_fit.label: High-return compounder
applicability_tests: Owner earnings can be separated from stock compensation, working capital, and maintenance capital.
Incremental returns on retained capital are observable and durable.
The competitive advantage explains pricing, retention, share, or cost outcomes.
Terminal value is supported by a reinvestment runway rather than an unexplained multiple.
failure_modes: growth projected without its capital cost
terminal value unsupported by a durable mechanism

validation_cohort.purpose: Test depletion, commodity exposure, finite reserves, and royalty-unit reconciliation.
Test tangible capital, credit losses, funding, regulation, and excess-capital realization.
Test owner earnings, capital-light reinvestment, cyclicality, and buyback value creation.
Test normalized margins, industry supply response, replacement cost, and reinvestment timing.
Test product concentration, pipeline probabilities, patent cliffs, and catalyst timing.

method_fit.rule: Use one primary method and no more than two corroborating methods; irrelevant methods and personas remain silent.

outcomes.weighting_rule: Do not change a persona's relevance within a power zone until at least 20 attributable, dividend-aware outcomes exist there; never reweight automatically.

attribution.explanation: The current component schedule is the first decision-grade baseline; attribution begins with the next dated valuation snapshot.

component_valuation.decision_rule: Use the complete low/base/high component schedule. Embedded components are estimated but not added twice.

timeline.label: Operating Cash Flow moved +37.6% versus the prior period (1,332.7 to 1,833.9).
Basic EPS moved +17.8% versus the prior period (14.6 to 17.1).
Q1 2026: net income $274.1M (+87% YoY); diluted EPS $5.41; strong Global Housing and Connected Living growth
FY2025 results: net income $872.7M (+15%); Global Housing Adjusted EBITDA $858.7M (+28%); Global Lifestyle $801.3M (+4%)
Issued $300M 5.55% senior notes due 2036; redeemed all $175M 6.10% senior notes due 2026 — term out debt at lower coupon
Completed exit from Global Preneed segment; portfolio now two operating segments (Global Lifestyle and Global Housing)
Accelerated share repurchases; diluted share count declined from 53.2M (FY2023) to 51.1M (FY2025)
Expanded Connected Living mobile device solutions globally; mobile attach became majority of Lifestyle revenue (53% FY2025)
Rebranded as Assurant; sharpened focus on housing and lifestyle specialty insurance via B2B2C partner distribution
Spun off from Fortis; listed on NYSE as AIZ — specialty insurance and extended protection franchise

industry.structure: Specialty insurance and extended protection distributed through B2B2C partnerships with carriers, lenders, retailers, and OEMs. Assurant holds leading positions in mobile device protection (Connected Living), vehicle service contracts (Global Automotive), and lender-placed homeowners insurance. Concentrated partner relationships create scale but also concentration risk.
industry.share_shift: Assurant gaining in Connected Living via global mobile carrier programs and financial-services cross-sell; Global Housing benefiting from lender-placed policy growth and lower catastrophe years; traditional extended service contract mix declining as mobile attach rises.
industry.trend: Mobile device lifecycle services and embedded insurance growing; lender-placed housing sensitive to catastrophe seasons and mortgage volumes; investment portfolio yields support net investment income ($527M FY2025); debt refinanced at lower coupons.

top_claims.claim: EPS (basic) growth moved from +103.9% to +91.3% YoY for two consecutive quarters. Source: SEC XBRL quarterly filings.
Growth regime upshift: YoY growth moved to +28.1% to +91.3% YoY (baseline median +3.8% YoY) for two consecutive quarters. Source: SEC XBRL quarterly filings.
MEIER KEITH reported an insider sale of 25,000 shares ($6,357,750).

Assurant is a partner-distributed specialty insurer with leading positions in mobile device protection and lender-placed homeowners insurance. FY2025 net income per share is about $17.09; at ~$275 (July 2026), total synthesis implies 8.07% per year over 7 years on filing-based owner cash. Moat is stable (embedded distr

risk_claims.claim: MEIER KEITH reported an insider sale of 25,000 shares ($6,357,750).

*Primary risk: Global Housing earnings are catastrophe-sensitive; a return to above-average reportable catastrophes would compress Housing Adjusted EBITDA and net income faster than Lifestyle growth offsets (`10-K_20260219_rpt20251231_acc0001267238_26_000010.htm` MD&A).

Partner concentration: Connected Living depends on major carrier renewals; loss of a top mobile partner would remove a double-digit revenue slice (`10-K_20260219_rpt20251231_acc0001267238_26_000010.htm` Item 1A).
